definition.text: China is Party-State polity in East Asia and the world's most populous major state. Governed by a single-party system under the Chinese Communist Party, it has transformed over the past four decades from a largely agrarian economy into a global industrial and technological power. China plays a central role in global manufacturing, trade, infrastructure development, and emerging technologies.

works.title: Actor Set Evolution of China
works.description: Temporal mapping of China’s actor space from imperial dynasties and bureaucratic elites to revolutionary transformation under the Chinese Communist Party, followed by reform-era technocratic governance and contemporary state-capitalist institutional structures.

works.title: Industrial and Technology Network Structure
works.description: Network analysis of China’s manufacturing clusters, state-owned enterprises, private technology firms, export logistics networks, and global infrastructure initiatives such as the Belt and Road.

works.title: Regional Spatial-Administrative Structure
works.description: Geospatial representation of China's provinces, major metropolitan clusters, industrial corridors, port systems, and internal economic zones.
